agent_raw: The proposed fraud model deployment for automatic order holds raises serious evaluation concerns. The backtest has three meaningful gaps. First, transactions already blocked by existing rules were excluded from training and evaluation — this is standard in model development but means the evaluation population may not perfectly match the live queue the model will score in production. Second, the mature-label period used for evaluation explicitly excludes recent holiday transactions because outcomes take weeks to resolve. The model has not been evaluated on the specific seasonal patterns it will face in the proposed deployment window. Third, the deployment decision is about automatic holds — a high-stakes action that delays legitimate customer orders. During the holiday season, false holds have asymmetric impact: missed delivery deadlines, order cancellations, and customer churn are real costs not captured by AUC or false positive rates. The backtest is a reasonable model development step but does not adequately support the decision to auto-hold orders without a shadow deployment phase that characterizes actual hold rates on live holiday traffic and quantifies customer harm.
all_issues_raised: Excluded pre-blocked transactions shift evaluation population from deployment population
Mature-label period excludes holiday-season fraud patterns relevant to deployment
Customer harm from false holds not quantified — only false positive rate reported
